---
schema: verdi.decisionconflict/v1
covers: 796a2b9447959162cff33dee48a1b5edf05c0eab
findings:
  - { id: judged-dcj-1, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-3 deliberately widens the preflight to feature scope, contradicting the parent feature's ac-1 which names only 'a named story'. The story flags this for seam review but declares no supersedes/exempts edge against the parent decision/AC it widens — the widening currently rests on an informal 'flagged for ratification' note rather than a declared edge, leaving the contradiction with closure-ergonomics ac-1's literal scope undeclared in the edge graph. (confidence 0.55)", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }
  - { id: judged-dcj-2, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-1 dispatches --preflight BEFORE the CI-only/--force-local publish guard, so a local preflight can report the closure gate fully green while an actual `verdi close` from the same checkout would still refuse (at the guard). Parent dc-5's rationale is that 'the preflight report and close's refusal are one code path and their required agreement (co-3) is structural' — deliberately exempting one of close's refusal paths from the preflight breaks that structural agreement for the guard-refusal case, and no exempts edge against dc-5/co-3 is declared for it. (confidence 0.40)", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }
  - { id: judged-dcj-3, kind: judged, text: "Story dc-2 bounds the preflight strictly to the closure-gate conditions and explicitly excludes rehearsing downstream ritual steps (align-freeze, archive commit), while parent dc-5 frames the mode as 'one verb owns the ritual and its rehearsal'. If dc-5's 'rehearsal' is read as covering the ritual rather than only the gate, dc-2 narrows the parent decision without a declared narrowing/exempts edge; the story argues from closure-ergonomics dc-1's gate enumeration but never declares the relationship to dc-5's broader wording. (confidence 0.30)", target_ref: spec/closure-ergonomics }

The story flags this for seam review but declares no supersedes/exempts edge against the parent decision/AC it widens — the widening currently rests on an informal 'flagged for ratification' note rather than a declared edge, leaving the contradiction with closure-ergonomics ac-1's literal scope undeclared in the edge graph.\",\"confidence\":0.55,\"target\":\"spec/closure-ergonomics\"},{\"id\":\"dcj-2\",\"text\":\"Story dc-1 dispatches --preflight BEFORE the CI-only/--force-local publish guard, so a local preflight can report the closure gate fully green while an actual `verdi close` from the same checkout would still refuse (at the guard). Parent dc-5's rationale is that 'the preflight report and close's refusal are one code path and their required agreement (co-3) is structural' — deliberately exempting one of close's refusal paths from the preflight breaks that structural agreement for the guard-refusal case, and no exempts edge against dc-5/co-3 is declared for it.\",\"confidence\":0.4,\"target\":\"spec/closure-ergonomics\"},{\"id\":\"dcj-3\",\"text\":\"Story dc-2 bounds the preflight strictly to the closure-gate conditions and explicitly excludes rehearsing downstream ritual steps (align-freeze, archive commit), while parent dc-5 frames the mode as 'one verb owns the ritual and its rehearsal'. If dc-5's 'rehearsal' is read as covering the ritual rather than only the gate, dc-2 narrows the parent decision without a declared narrowing/exempts edge; the story argues from closure-ergonomics dc-1's gate enumeration but never declares the relationship to dc-5's broader wording.\",\"confidence\":0.3,\"target\":\"spec/closure-ergonomics\"}]}" }

# Decision-conflict report

## Computed (declared-edge completeness)

(none)

## Judged (undeclared-conflict sweep)

- **judged-dcj-1** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-3 deliberately widens the preflight to feature scope, contradicting the parent feature's ac-1 which names only 'a named story'. The story flags this for seam review but declares no supersedes/exempts edge against the parent decision/AC it widens — the widening currently rests on an informal 'flagged for ratification' note rather than a declared edge, leaving the contradiction with closure-ergonomics ac-1's literal scope undeclared in the edge graph. (confidence 0.55)
- **judged-dcj-2** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-1 dispatches --preflight BEFORE the CI-only/--force-local publish guard, so a local preflight can report the closure gate fully green while an actual `verdi close` from the same checkout would still refuse (at the guard). Parent dc-5's rationale is that 'the preflight report and close's refusal are one code path and their required agreement (co-3) is structural' — deliberately exempting one of close's refusal paths from the preflight breaks that structural agreement for the guard-refusal case, and no exempts edge against dc-5/co-3 is declared for it. (confidence 0.40)
- **judged-dcj-3** [UNDISPOSITIONED]: Story dc-2 bounds the preflight strictly to the closure-gate conditions and explicitly excludes rehearsing downstream ritual steps (align-freeze, archive commit), while parent dc-5 frames the mode as 'one verb owns the ritual and its rehearsal'. If dc-5's 'rehearsal' is read as covering the ritual rather than only the gate, dc-2 narrows the parent decision without a declared narrowing/exempts edge; the story argues from closure-ergonomics dc-1's gate enumeration but never declares the relationship to dc-5's broader wording. (confidence 0.30)
